items.reason: GERD refractory to PPI - endoscopy consideration
items.clinical_notes: Patient with persistent heartburn despite omeprazole 20mg daily for 8 weeks. No alarm symptoms. Dietary modifications attempted. Requesting EGD evaluation.

items.message_text: Dr. O'Brien, referring Ms. Sophie Laurent for GI evaluation. She has persistent GERD symptoms despite 8 weeks of omeprazole 20mg daily with appropriate timing (30 min before breakfast). She describes daily heartburn and occasional regurgitation, worse postprandially. No dysphagia, weight loss, or GI bleeding. I have increased her to omeprazole 40mg BID and reinforced dietary modifications. Would EGD be warranted at this point given her refractory symptoms?

Dr. Chen, thank you for the referral. Given the failure of standard-dose PPI therapy with appropriate administration and no alarm features, an EGD is warranted to evaluate for erosive esophagitis, Barrett's esophagus, or eosinophilic esophagitis. I would also like to assess for hiatal hernia. If the EGD is unremarkable, we may consider ambulatory pH monitoring to confirm the diagnosis. I will reach out to the patient to schedule the procedure. Please ensure authorization is in place.
